commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a case-preserving session-key registry for Matrix channel/group and Signal group peers, updates session construction/store lookup/gateway explicit-key handling, and adds session-key regression tests.

PR surface: Source +105, Tests +167. Total +272 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path for the PR defect by placing two Matrix session keys in the store whose room IDs differ only by case, then updating the mixed-case key so the folded sibling is collected in `legacyKeys` and deleted during persistence.
commands.review_followup: The blocker is a narrow mechanical repair in session-store alias cleanup with a clear regression test, so it is suitable for an automated fix attempt on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve Matrix room-id case in session keys (#75670)
target.body:
Fixes #75670.

## Summary

Matrix room IDs (and thread event IDs) are opaque and **case-sensitive** per the Matrix spec, but OpenClaw's session-key canonicalization lowercased them. That forked one room into duplicate sessions and produced `403 M_FORBIDDEN` on recovery/delivery paths that reconstruct the target from the (lowercased) session key — even though `deliveryContext.to` stayed correct. This is the room-ID side of #75670 (the issue's thread-event side was already addressed by the injected identity thread-normalizer; the room-ID/session-key layer was not).

Rather than a Matrix-specific patch, this introduces a **generic, opt-in case-preservation registry** that defaults to today's lowercase behavior and lets channels enroll individually. Signal group preservation (#82853) is encoded to match its prior behavior exactly; Matrix `channel`/`group` is the newly-enrolled channel. Un-enrolled channels are byte-for-byte unchanged.

## What changed

- **`CASE_PRESERVING_PEERS` registry + `isCasePreservingPeer(channel, peerKind)`** in `session-key-utils.ts` — single policy source. `signal/group` = `segment` span (unscoped, behavior-identical); `matrix/channel|group` = `tail` span (opaque room id incl. embedded `:server`, plus any `:thread:<event>` suffix).
- Consulted at the three sites that lowercased the room id:
  - construction — `normalizeSessionPeerId`
  - store canonicalization — `normalizeSessionKeyPreservingOpaquePeerIds` (rewritten to collect preserved spans and lowercase only the complement, anchored to the agent-scoped head)
  - gateway send — explicit `request.sessionKey` now uses the opaque-preserving normalizer instead of raw lowercase
- **Exact mixed-case wins over folded legacy** in `resolveSessionStoreEntry` and `delivery-info.ts` lookup (exact ranks below routability, above freshness). Legacy lowercased rows collapse toward the exact key on the next write — no orphaning (read-time fallback) and no permanent duplicate.
- Matrix DM/MXID and all non-enrolled channels keep the default lowercase behavior (out of scope by design).

Known follow-up (documented, not addressed here): a thread key built off a `main` base (`agent:<a>:main:thread:<event>`) has no `<channel>:<peerKind>:` boundary, so store-canon can't identify the owning channel from the key and still lowercases that event. It is the main-session thread shape, not the room-session shape behind this issue.

## Related / prior work

Complements/extends earlier case-handling work rather than duplicating it:

- **#78206** (closed) / **#77205** (merged, `ceca7fdf`) — fixed lowercased room IDs on the *direct send* path; this PR addresses the residual **session-key layer** (construction, store-canon, gateway explicit-key, lookup precedence) that still produced recovery/identity `403`s after #77205.
- **#57337** (closed, unmerged) — an earlier attempt to preserve case in group/channel peer IDs for session keys; revisited here as a generic opt-in registry with read-time precedence and live two-room proof.
- **#63828 / #64343** (merged) — preserved Matrix ACP thread-binding targets; same opaque-id-case class, different surface.
- **#82853** (merged) — Signal group session-id preservation; generalized here into the shared registry, with Matrix newly enrolled.
- Cross-channel evidence for the *generic* (not Matrix-only) approach: **#81704** (LINE recipients), **#75261 / #71798** (cron Matrix room IDs), **#82141 / #75303** (cron LINE/channel casing).

The issue's thread-event-id symptom is already handled on live Matrix paths via an injected identity thread-normalizer; the residual `main:thread:<event>` case is the documented follow-up above.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Matrix room IDs were lowercased into session keys (`agent:<a>:matrix:channel:<lowercased-room-id>`), forking sessions and causing `403 M_FORBIDDEN` on send/recovery paths that rebuild the target from the key, even though the bot is joined to the real mixed-case room.
- **Real environment tested:** A long-running production OpenClaw gateway (self-hosted Matrix homeserver, multi-account, macOS) running this exact patch on top of current `main`. Gateway reloaded onto the patched build; two rooms that had logged the bug were exercised with live traffic. (Room IDs / names redacted below; both are mixed-case opaque IDs.)
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:** (1) built the patched gateway and reloaded the LaunchDaemon; (2) confirmed the running process is on the patched build; (3) let organic traffic hit room A and sent a fresh human message into room B; (4) inspected the live session store and gateway delivery logs for both rooms; (5) ran the existing matrix-403 watcher.
- **Evidence after fix (live prod):**
  - **Room A** (a mixed-case Matrix room, redacted): the persisted session key is now a single mixed-case `agent:<a>:matrix:channel:<MixedRoomA>` — the legacy lowercased row collapsed (one top-level key, not two). Zero case-bug `403`s since the reload; the matrix-403 watcher reports 0 case-bug events.
  - **Room B** (a second mixed-case Matrix room, redacted): a live message that **`403`'d before the patch** (`[403] User … not in room <lowercased-RoomB>`) now delivers cleanly after the patch — gateway log: `setting pendingEvent status to sent in <MixedRoomB> … -> $<eventId>`, and the new persisted channel session key is mixed-case `agent:<a>:matrix:channel:<MixedRoomB>`.
  - Deterministic harness driving the real production functions (no mocks): mixed-case room id preserved through construction + store-canon; case-distinct rooms no longer collapse to one key; controls confirm Signal (scoped + unscoped), non-Matrix channels, Matrix DM, and `:thread:`-shaped non-Matrix peer ids are all unchanged.
- **Observed result after fix:** outbound sends to mixed-case Matrix rooms succeed where they previously `403`'d; session lookup/recovery/status see the correct mixed-case key; one room = one session (no fork). Non-Matrix channels and Signal behave exactly as before.
- **What was not tested:** the documented `main:thread` residual above (left for follow-up); Windows/Linux runtime (logic is platform-independent). `pnpm check` passes except a pre-existing, unrelated `npm shrinkwrap guard` failure about AWS-SDK lockfile/node_modules version skew — this PR touches no `package.json`/lockfile.

Supplemental (not a substitute for the above): `pnpm build` clean, `pnpm tsgo:core:test` clean, and 311/311 targeted tests pass including a new regression matrix in `session-key-case-preservation.test.ts` (synthetic room fixtures, Signal scoped/unscoped guards, non-Matrix/DM/`:thread:`-peer controls, and the documented residual).

## AI-assisted disclosure 🤖

AI-assisted (Claude). The diff was developed and reviewed iteratively with a local `codex review` loop (multiple rounds on the plan + a final review on the diff, ending in AGREE). The real-behavior proof above is from a human-operated production gateway, not generated. Happy to share the plan/review notes if useful.

I've enabled **Allow edits by maintainers**.
target.author: lukeboyett
